real_bugs.description: Loop condition i <= users.length should be i < users.length, causes TypeError on undefined access
JSON.parse throws SyntaxError on malformed input with no error handling
String.replace with string arg only removes first occurrence, case-sensitive, ignores other XSS vectors
No res.ok check before res.json(), 4xx/5xx responses parsed as valid data or throw

debatable.description: Returns Infinity/NaN on zero division — standard IEEE 754 behavior, context-dependent
No try-catch for network failures — Promise rejection propagates naturally
Uses any[] parameter — code quality issue, not runtime bug
